auth.label: API Key
auth.description: Emailable API key sent with the Authorization Bearer header. Create or roll it from your Emailable dashboard API settings: https://emailable.com/docs/api.

actions.name: get_account_info
actions.description: Retrieve Emailable account information including owner email and available credits.

inputSchema.description: No input is required for this action.

owner_email.description: The email address of the Emailable account owner.

available_credits.description: The number of verification credits currently available.

outputSchema.description: The Emailable account information returned by the account endpoint.

actions.name: get_batch_status
actions.description: Retrieve the latest Emailable status and results for an existing batch verification job.

batch_id.description: The unique batch identifier returned by Emailable batch creation.

inputSchema.description: Input payload for retrieving the status of an Emailable batch verification job.

id.description: The unique batch identifier returned by Emailable batch creation.

message.description: The current status message for the Emailable batch.

processed.description: The number of emails processed so far in the batch.

total.description: The total number of emails submitted in the batch.

email.description: The normalized email address verified by Emailable.

state.description: The verification state returned by Emailable.

reason.description: The provider reason returned by Emailable for the verification result.

score.description: The Emailable deliverability score from 0 to 100.

user.description: The local part of the verified email address.

domain.description: The domain part of the verified email address.

free.description: Whether the email is hosted by a free email provider.

role.description: Whether the email is a role-based address such as support@ or info@.

accept_all.description: Whether the domain appears to accept all inbound email addresses.

disposable.description: Whether the email belongs to a disposable or temporary email provider.

did_you_mean.description: Suggested corrected email address when Emailable detects a likely typo.

mx_record.description: The MX record used during verification.

smtp_provider.description: The SMTP provider identified for the email domain.

no_reply.description: Whether the address is a no-reply address.

mailbox_full.description: Whether the mailbox appears to be full.

first_name.description: The first name inferred from the email address, when available.

last_name.description: The last name inferred from the email address, when available.

full_name.description: The full name inferred from the email address, when available.

gender.description: The gender inferred from the email address, when available.

tag.description: The tag portion of the email address, such as the value after a plus sign.

items.description: The single-email verification result returned by Emailable.

emails.description: The individual email verification results when Emailable returns inline batch results.

download_file.description: The download URL for large batch result exports, when Emailable returns one.

total_counts.description: Summary counts grouped by verification state or provider reason for the Emailable batch.

reason_counts.description: Summary counts grouped by verification state or provider reason for the Emailable batch.

outputSchema.description: The batch status payload returned by Emailable.

actions.name: verify_batch_emails
actions.description: Create an Emailable batch verification job for a list of email addresses.

items.description: An email address included in the batch verification request.

emails.description: The list of email addresses to verify in the Emailable batch request.

inputSchema.description: Input payload for creating an Emailable batch verification job.

id.description: The unique batch identifier returned by Emailable batch creation.

message.description: The status message returned after Emailable creates the batch.

outputSchema.description: The response returned after Emailable creates a batch verification job.

actions.name: verify_email
actions.description: Verify a single email address and return Emailable deliverability signals.

email.description: The email address to verify with Emailable.

inputSchema.description: Input payload for verifying a single email address with Emailable.

email.description: The normalized email address verified by Emailable.

state.description: The verification state returned by Emailable.

reason.description: The provider reason returned by Emailable for the verification result.

score.description: The Emailable deliverability score from 0 to 100.

user.description: The local part of the verified email address.

domain.description: The domain part of the verified email address.

free.description: Whether the email is hosted by a free email provider.

role.description: Whether the email is a role-based address such as support@ or info@.

accept_all.description: Whether the domain appears to accept all inbound email addresses.

disposable.description: Whether the email belongs to a disposable or temporary email provider.

did_you_mean.description: Suggested corrected email address when Emailable detects a likely typo.

mx_record.description: The MX record used during verification.

smtp_provider.description: The SMTP provider identified for the email domain.

no_reply.description: Whether the address is a no-reply address.

mailbox_full.description: Whether the mailbox appears to be full.

first_name.description: The first name inferred from the email address, when available.

last_name.description: The last name inferred from the email address, when available.

full_name.description: The full name inferred from the email address, when available.

gender.description: The gender inferred from the email address, when available.

tag.description: The tag portion of the email address, such as the value after a plus sign.

outputSchema.description: The single-email verification result returned by Emailable.
